sources.label: Yubico IR - OpenAI TAC passkey mandate partnership (Jun 1, 2026)
StockTitan - Yubico backs OpenAI passkey mandate for TAC users
Placera - OpenAI kraver passkeys i TAC-program (Jun 2, 2026)
MarketScreener - Yubico Q1 2026 profit collapse (rev -23% y/y)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; today's -4.55% is a flow-driven fade of the ~21% OpenAI-partnership pop (Jun 1), not a thesis break. The agentic-identity thesis was just validated by OpenAI's TAC passkey mandate, and the pullback to 54.5 SEK with YTD -25% modestly restores entry asymmetry on this under-owned $0.54B name, so a small positive drift without a rank change relative to yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: OpenAI mandating hardware-backed passkeys for its most privileged frontier-model users sets an industry precedent that directly monetizes Yubico's core product as agentic-AI security infrastructure; subscription revenue (+13.8% y/y) and ARR (+11% to 383.5 MSEK) show the recurring base compounding beneath weak headline numbers.
verdicts.neutral: The OpenAI TAC program is small in revenue terms today; the partnership is a credibility signal, not a disclosed contract. Stock has round-tripped much of the pop and sits near pre-news levels, leaving the setup roughly where yesterday's hold left it.
verdicts.bear: Q1 2026 revenue fell 23% y/y with EBIT collapsing to 14.9 MSEK and an 11% revenue miss; if hardware key demand keeps shrinking faster than subscriptions grow, the agentic-identity option is being funded by a deteriorating core business.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Follow-on AI-lab or hyperscaler passkey mandates (or a disclosed OpenAI volume order) converting the June 1 precedent into bookings, plus Q2 2026 report showing hardware revenue stabilization.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued double-digit hardware revenue decline and enterprise spending softness swamping ARR growth, with platform passkeys (device-bound, software) eroding the hardware-key niche.
